title: Pternandra coriacea
taxonomy: Pternandra coriacea is a species of flowering plant in the family Melastomataceae, commonly known as the Melastome family. This family is a group of flowering plants that are native to tropical and subtropical regions around the world. The Melastomataceae family includes over 1,300 species, and Pternandra coriacea is one of the many species within this family. Pternandra coriacea is native to the Pacific Islands, particularly in the tropical regions of Australia, New Guinea, and nearby islands. The classification of Pternandra coriacea makes it a part of the larger family Melastomataceae, which is further classified under the order Myrtales.

intro: Pternandra coriacea, also known as the Malay apple, is a species of flowering plant in the family Melastomataceae. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Pternandra coriacea include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
